🍕 From $10,000 to a Global Empire  
  
In 1959, Marian Ilitch and her husband, Mike Ilitch, made a bold decision. With seven kids at home and just $10,000 in savings, they started a small pizza shop called Little Caesars in Detroit. There was no safety net—just belief and hard work. For years, growth was slow. The dream seemed fragile.  
  
Then came a simple but powerful idea.  
  
In 1979, they introduced “Pizza! Pizza!”—two pizzas for the price of one. It wasn’t just a deal; it was a game-changer. Within five years, their stores exploded to thousands across the country, turning Little Caesars into one of America’s biggest pizza chains. What started as a struggle became a nationwide success story.  
  
But Marian’s journey didn’t stop there.  
  
The Ilitch family went on to own the Detroit Red Wings and Detroit Tigers. After Mike’s passing in 2017, Marian took full control at age 84, becoming one of the few women to own multiple major sports franchises.  
  
From a small kitchen dream…  
to a global legacy.  
  
She proved that patience, resilience, and one smart idea can change everything.
